design_philosophy.core_loop: Best-of-three fighting matches against progressively difficult opponents; players execute special moves and manage spirit gauge resource during combat
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Execute learned combos and special moves (Desperation Moves) while managing limited spirit meter before opponent depletes your health
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master a martial arts fighting style (Kyokugenryu Karate) as part of a personal quest narrative; progress from street fighter to defeating crime bosses
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Integrates story progression into fighting game structure where narrative (kidnapped sister, facing father as final boss) is central to campaign mode; emphasizes taunt system and spirit management as strategic mechanics rather than pure execution

difficulty.continues_system: Player can continue after losing a match in arcade/campaign progression
difficulty.checkpointing: Match-based progression; advancing to next opponent upon victory
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players unlock special techniques through bonus games (spirit gauge expansion via 'Beer Bottle Cut' minigame) that provide strategic depth for subsequent fights

progression.upgrade_system: Special move acquisition through bonus games between fights; spirit gauge enhancement; taunt system for psychological gameplay
progression.unlock_mechanism: Completing story mode with Ryo or Robert unlocks playable Mr. Big and Mr. Karate (in later versions/arcade); eight of ten fighters available in versus mode by default
replayability_hooks: Two playable story characters (Ryo Sakazaki and Robert Garcia) with potentially different fight sequences
Eight-fighter versus mode roster accessible from start
Bonus games to increase maximum spirit gauge between story matches
Multiple difficulty settings implied through continue system

world_building_techniques: Story context provided before campaign: kidnapped sister Yuri by crime boss Mr. Big; personal vendetta against organized crime
Character relationships: Ryo's father (Takuma/Mr. Karate) as final antagonist twist (cliff-hanger ending)
Setting in 1978 within SNK fictional universe shared with Fatal Fury series
Narrative framing of fights as progression toward rescue/confrontation rather than tournament brackets

boss_design.philosophy: Progressive skill gates; final boss (Mr. Karate/Takuma) represents thematic climax where player faces father figure with narrative weight; intermediate bosses (Mr. Big) serve plot function beyond mechanical challenge
notable_bosses: Mr. Big - crime boss antagonist; mid-campaign confrontation
Mr. Karate (Takuma Sakazaki) - final boss; revealed to be Ryo's father; thematic centerpiece

faction_indicators: Mr. Big's criminal organization - antagonistic faction opposing protagonists
Kyokugenryu Karate practitioners - player faction identity
danger_signals: Sister kidnapping as motivating incident for protagonist
Final boss identity twist (father as antagonist) suggests family/loyalty conflict themes
environmental_storytelling: Opponent stages reflect their identity: Jack's stage environment named 'Mac's Bar' (changed to 'Cafe' in US/EU due to content policies)
Character selection and progression implicitly communicate social hierarchy (street fighters vs. crime bosses)

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Art of Fighting (SNES)
